title: magnolia sabahensis
intro: Magnolia sabahensis, also known as the Sabah magnolia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Magnoliaceae, native to Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Magnolia sabahensis often include its bark, leaves, and flowers, which are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its therapeutic properties.
